determinism_evidence: Single threshold compare; no internal state.
perceptual_basis: Sub-bass below ~15% energy is inaudible to most hearing aids; gate prevents idle ring spawn.

determinism_evidence: Linear function of one scalar; no random.
perceptual_basis: Higher sub-bass density => faster pulse cadence (perceptual: louder bass feels faster).
Loudness => visual weight; preserves linearity between input intensity and visual emphasis.

determinism_evidence: HSB lerp with shortest-path hue interpolation.
perceptual_basis: Quiet sub-bass reads as gravity (atmosphere); loud sub-bass takes the song's primary mood color.

transform: intensity = max(B[0] * bandMask[0], breath * 0.25); breath = 0.5 + 0.5*sin(t*2pi*(0.4 + A*0.5))
determinism_evidence: Closed-form in (B[0], wall clock, A); breath is purely time-driven.
perceptual_basis: Sub-bass overrides slow heartbeat-rate breath when loud; floor stays alive when bass is silent.

transform: mass = min(1, B[1]*bandMask[1] + B[2]*bandMask[2]*0.5); gate at mass > 0.04
perceptual_basis: Bass body is felt mass; gate prevents idle volumetrics in silent passages.

determinism_evidence: Per-frame multiplication; bottom layers stay anchored.
perceptual_basis: Embodiment: louder bass lifts the felt mass off the floor; lower layers anchor for grounding.
Bass causes the floor to glow rather than stay matte; perceptual reinforcement of impact.

transform: contributes 0.5 * B[2] * bandMask[2] to mass scalar
perceptual_basis: Low-mid extends bass-body upward without claiming a separate texture.

transform: midEnergy = (B[2]*m[2] + B[3]*m[3] + B[4]*m[4]*0.6)/3; gate = midEnergy * HR; emit if gate > 0.15
determinism_evidence: Algebraic combination of 3 bands and HR; no state.
perceptual_basis: Mids carry melody; harmonic-weighted gate prevents bleeds during noise washes.

determinism_evidence: Linear; clamped to 0.30s minimum at gate=1.0.
perceptual_basis: Stronger melodic mid energy => faster bleed cadence.

determinism_evidence: max() of weighted bands; no random.
perceptual_basis: Brilliance/presence map to ribbon prominence in the SKY zone.
Below ~4% ultra-high there is effectively no shimmer to render.

transform: radius = (0.7 + B[7] * 2.4 * emphasis) * envelope; emphasis in {0.45, 1.0}
determinism_evidence: Closed-form per speck; emphasis fixed at spawn.
perceptual_basis: Loud cymbal => bigger sparkle; matches perceptual size of the transient.

transform: alpha = clamp((0.20 + 0.70*B[7]) * emphasis * envelope, 0, alphaCap); alphaCap = 0.80 (chorus/drop) else 0.55
determinism_evidence: Per-frame multiplication and clamp; cap is a section constant.
perceptual_basis: Hard upper bound prevents Frost from outshouting volumetric textures.
Loud songs feel brighter overall without any specific texture having to fire.
Spectral disorder maps to noise opacity; section dialect supplies the floor.

determinism_evidence: Single uniform value derived from opacity.
perceptual_basis: Decouples shader-internal jitter amplitude from rendered alpha.
Spectral instability bends the scene like heat distortion.

transform: intensity = max(0.35, F) (snapshot at burst time)
perceptual_basis: Stronger spikes produce visibly brighter slashes.

transform: emit 3 + int(F*5) shards on each counter increment
determinism_evidence: Detector uses adaptive threshold + cooldown; same flux trace => same spike timestamps.
perceptual_basis: Snare/clap-class transients that BeatEvents miss; third discrete event channel.

transform: u = uniform(0.05, 0.95); v = uniform(0.46, 0.54); angle = uniform(-pi/6, pi/6)
determinism_evidence: Spike timing deterministic; bounded random within HORIZON_BAND for layout.
perceptual_basis: Slashes confined to HORIZON_BAND so the event channel has a fixed visual address.

determinism_evidence: CN deterministically chooses zone; +/-0.08 jitter is bounded cosmetic.
perceptual_basis: Embodiment: kick (CN ~ 0.1) erupts near v=0.78; hihat (CN ~ 0.9) flashes near v=0.22.

transform: u = clamp(activeULo + CN*range + frac(seed*3.1)*0.3 - 0.15, activeULo, activeUHi)
determinism_evidence: CN biases u; jitter via deterministic golden-ratio frac of onset.time => identical playback => identical u.
perceptual_basis: Brighter onsets pan right; per-onset spread is reproducible across replays.

transform: v_center = 0.05 + 0.25 * (1 - CN); +/- 0.06 v jitter at respawn
determinism_evidence: Centroid choice of zone deterministic; per-respawn jitter only within +/-6% v.
perceptual_basis: Brighter spectrum lifts specks toward v=0.05; duller spectrum sinks them to ~0.30.

transform: activeIdx = CN * 7.0 (continuous); per-rung brightness via gaussian falloff
perceptual_basis: Discrete 8-rung dashboard reads centroid as a level meter.
Bright spectrum + harmonic music pushes the lattice cluster upward.
Persistent dashboard: 'song is in F# right now' = which stop is glowing.

determinism_evidence: Stop palette is fixed function of CS; positions never move.
perceptual_basis: 12 pitch classes hold fixed positions so position becomes a learnable code.

determinism_evidence: Modular addition; identical H => identical triad.
perceptual_basis: Iridescent thin-film triad encodes the song's harmonic root.
Tonal music spins the triad faster; rotation speed encodes confidence.
Key modulation pans the melodic body horizontally; left/right drift = chord change.

determinism_evidence: Hue lerp shortest-path; identical inputs => identical outputs.
perceptual_basis: Tonal passages tint the bass mass with the song's key.

transform: if CS > 0.4: birthHue = HSB(H, 0.85, 1.0); else: birthHue = scheme.accent
determinism_evidence: Discrete branch on a single threshold.
perceptual_basis: Tonal attacks ignite in the song's key; noisy attacks ignite in the emotional accent.

determinism_evidence: Lerp blend after deterministic cycle interpolation.
perceptual_basis: Ribbons take the key tint when tonal; pure scheme cycle when atonal.
Ultra-high tonal => faint key tint on the shimmer; noisy => near-white.

transform: if CS > 0.5: core = lerp(scheme.accent, HSB(H, 0.85, 1.0), 0.30); else: core = scheme.accent
perceptual_basis: Rays as 'punch' carry the key's color when tonal.
Downbeats are emphasis; key tint makes the emphasis specific.
Even the deepest layer carries a residue of the song's key when tonal.
Disappears in atonal sections so harmonic/atonal boundary is visible on the frame.

transform: draw if CS > 0.05; rail saturation = 0.65 + 0.20 * CS
perceptual_basis: Atonal music => grayscale rail; rail stops shouting a key it can't read.

transform: each hue-pull texture multiplies its tint amount by CS * factor (factor in {0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6})
perceptual_basis: Tonality scales how much every texture borrows the key's color.

transform: emit on onset only if onset.sharpness < 0.50 AND HR > 0.35
perceptual_basis: Slow attacks in harmonic context spawn bleeds; sharp/noisy onsets become Embers instead.
Pitched mids form round bleeds (HR=1 => 1.0); noise smears them sideways (HR=0 => 2.2).
Harmonic richness adds layered tonal energy; noise suppresses ribbon count.
Noise adds turbulence to the flow; harmony lets ribbons drift smoothly.

transform: swayAmp = 0.15 * (1 - HR); slosh = sin(t*0.3 + layer) * swayAmp
determinism_evidence: Time-driven sin with HR-controlled amplitude; deterministic in (t, HR).
perceptual_basis: Noise = lateral motion; harmony = stillness.
Cleaner spectrum produces a denser lattice; noise reduces it.
Edges fade to nothing as music turns inharmonic - structure literally dissolves.
Noise blurs the lattice into mush; harmony keeps cells crisp.
Origin traces pitch motion: rising melody => up-right anchor, falling => down-left.

transform: if PD > 0.08: -pi/2 dir, 0.55*pi half-cone; if PD < -0.08: +pi/2 dir, 0.55*pi half; else: 0 dir, pi half (radial)
perceptual_basis: Strong direction => directed cone; static pitch => symmetric radial bloom.
Rising pitch slows the drift ('smoke holds'); falling pitch accelerates settling.

transform: warmAlpha = V * A * 0.45; coolAlpha = (1 - V) * A * 0.45
perceptual_basis: Happy => warm diagonal lights up; sad => cool. Persistent compositional bias, not motion.
Anxiety/sadness gives noise an uncanny cool cast; neutral+ keeps grain colorless.

transform: vCenter = 0.5 - V * 0.25 + (1 - A) * 0.10 (clamped to active V bounds)
perceptual_basis: Positive valence lifts the bleed; low arousal settles it.
Arousal opens the corner blooms wider; calm contracts them.
Calm => 7.5s cycle; intense => 2.7s.
Discrete ray density encodes arousal level at a glance.

transform: sigma = 0.7 + 1.3 * A; brightness[i] = exp(-(i - activeIdx)^2 / sigma^2)
perceptual_basis: Calm = sharp single rung; intense = blurred wide spread.
Calm = 2.5s heartbeat; intense = 1.1s breath. The dark itself breathes with the song.

transform: palette = sum(weight_i * anchor_i HSB) for i in 4 biomes; anchors are constants
determinism_evidence: Hysteresis state explicit (dominantBiome, dominantBiomeSince); same (V,A) trajectory => same palette.
perceptual_basis: Single source of truth for color slots consumed by every texture downstream.

determinism_evidence: Pulse decay closed-form exponential of dt.
perceptual_basis: Faint sub-beats filtered out; only legible beats flash.

determinism_evidence: Branch on pulse magnitude (downbeat detection); radius linear in pulse.
perceptual_basis: Downbeats reach further visually; offbeats stay local.
Visible weight scales with beat strength; capped peak prevents overpowering the scene.

determinism_evidence: Counter increments deterministically when sortedOnsets[i].time <= currentTime.
perceptual_basis: Sharp transients become embers; slow attacks fall through to InkBleed.
Sharper attacks produce denser bursts.

determinism_evidence: Per-particle Random is the only stochastic component; envelope deterministic.
perceptual_basis: Sharper attacks eject faster; randomization spreads the burst (cosmetic).

transform: spawn 1 bleed if onset.sharpness < 0.50 AND HR > 0.35
perceptual_basis: Bowed strings, pads, sustained notes spread rather than strike.

transform: static Set<TextureID> per label (intro: 6, verse: 13, chorus: 17, bridge: 10, break: 6, drop: 17, outro: progress-interpolated)
determinism_evidence: Switch on label string; outro is the only progress-interpolated branch (deterministic in p).
perceptual_basis: Sections reshape the active texture set per song-structural region.

transform: static [Double] per label; outro multiplies by (1 - p*0.7) over progress
perceptual_basis: Section-specific frequency emphasis (e.g. break suppresses everything but sub-bass).

transform: per-section constants applied as palette-wide HSB multipliers / shifts
determinism_evidence: Linear HSB transforms with constants per section.
perceptual_basis: Choruses pop, bridges rotate hue, breaks desaturate.

transform: trigger on rising edge into 'drop' label OR (smoothedArousal>0.82 AND flux>0.75 AND energy>0.70); 4s cooldown
determinism_evidence: Explicit state machine with phaseStarted, lastTriggerTime, releaseGeneration counter.
perceptual_basis: Drop is a scored moment; dual trigger catches drops the section detector misses.

determinism_evidence: First-order IIR with fixed coefficients; same input sequence => same trajectory.
perceptual_basis: Cap at 0.55 prevents masking transients; EMA kills frame-jitter so the motor doesn't buzz.

determinism_evidence: Cursor-walked at AudioPlayer tick; userMul is a UserDefaults constant.
perceptual_basis: Downbeats need physical weight; clamp at 0.30 floor ensures even quiet downbeats are felt.
Downbeats feel sharper than offbeats.

determinism_evidence: Same multiplier convention as downbeat with reduced gain.
perceptual_basis: Offbeats lighter than downbeats; saturation prevention for the motor.
Slightly softer edge than downbeats.
Onsets and beats overlap; firing both at full strength saturates the motor.

determinism_evidence: max() of two scaled values; both deterministic from event payload.
perceptual_basis: Steeper attack envelope feels sharper; falls back to backend sharpness if slope is flat.

determinism_evidence: Drop trigger integrative-deterministic; pattern playback from static .ahap file.
perceptual_basis: Pre-authored haptic envelope syncs to the visual flash phase via a single shared callback.
